page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 dedicated virtual CPUs. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. This instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring secure and isolated virtualization.

This server configuration does not include any bundled local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage. The instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach high-performance persistent disks depending on their workload needs. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, prioritizing pure general-purpose compute density and memory over localized data caching or hardware-accelerated processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96as_v6 offers a high-density resource profile for workloads requiring substantial CPU and memory capacity without the premium cost associated with specialized GPU or high-speed local NVMe hardware. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server type suitable for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, web serving fleets, large-scale build systems, and intermediate database hosting where external storage latency is acceptable.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD x86_64 processor. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is fully capable of utilizing Premium Storage for external disk requirements. The lack of local storage and hardware accelerators represents a design tradeoff that optimizes the instance for cost-effective, high-density compute and memory allocation. This configuration is well-suited for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications, web serving, multi-threaded processing environments, and intermediate database hosting where external storage latency is acceptable.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

tagline: A high-density virtual machine offering ninety-six dedicated AMD cores and balanced memory without bundled local storage.

384.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads and web serving
